description:
Goal: derive backend-ready ABI and emission facts from a backend-owned executable-plan projection of the closed Compiler2 artifact surface. Signal: interpreter, JIT, and AOT adapters consume a settled, change-honest artifact program and never rediscover reachability, dispatch, return lanes, or type flow.

Blocked on the backend executable-plan projection: backend work resumes only after Compiler2 materialization publishes the backend-facing facts adapters need, rather than asking old-world planner/interpreter/codegen paths to infer them on demand.

Change:
- Add AbiReady and Emitted facts for interpreter, JIT, and AOT adapters from the closed backend artifact program.
- Codegen consumes callable entries, return contracts, effects, extern marshal facts, and allocation-relevant facts already handed over by the artifact projection.
- Backend adapters report unsupported or missing closed facts as fatal artifact errors; telemetry carries raw ids/facts, not formatted summaries.

Why:
- Codegen should lower settled facts, not decide reachability, dispatch, return lanes, or type flow.

Checks:
- Backend attempts to ask type/callee/reachability questions are fatal.
- Quicksort and Enum.reduce preserve the expected absence/presence and allocation contracts through telemetry plus runtime/artifact assertions.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: derive ABI and emission from closed facts
